questions: how many planets are in the solar system?
solar system e koyti graha ache?
answers: The universe is everything that exists — all of space, time, matter, and energy. It is estimated to be about 13.8 billion years old and is still expanding. The observable universe is about 93 billion light-years in diameter.

A galaxy is a massive system of stars, gas, dust, and dark matter bound together by gravity. Our galaxy, the Milky Way, contains an estimated 100-400 billion stars. There are billions of galaxies in the observable universe.

The Big Bang theory is the leading explanation for the origin of the universe. It states that the universe began as an extremely hot, dense point about 13.8 billion years ago and has been expanding and cooling ever since.

Our solar system consists of the Sun, 8 planets (Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune), dwarf planets like Pluto, moons, asteroids, and comets. It formed about 4.6 billion years ago from a cloud of gas and dust.

Stars are giant balls of hot gas, primarily hydrogen and helium. They produce energy through nuclear fusion in their cores, where hydrogen atoms fuse to form helium, releasing enormous amounts of light and heat.
Universe koto boro? Observable universe er diameter holo praye 93 billion light-years. Kintu actual universe aro boro hote pare — amra shudhu oi part ta dekhi jetar light amader kache poucheche.

Yes, the universe is expanding, and the expansion is actually accelerating! Scientists discovered this in 1998 by observing distant supernovae. A mysterious force called dark energy is believed to drive this acceleration.
Big Bang er pore universe thanda hote hote stars, galaxies, ar planets toiri hoyeche. Prothome hydrogen ar helium gas chilo, tarpor gravity er karone era ektre hoy ar stars form kore.
